items.reason: All 15 required decision areas (person/agent, learning context, roles, enrollment/membership, results/scores, standards alignment, identifiers, time/session, content/resource, tenancy, privacy surfaces, runtime coverage, audit response, static mirror, service-role) are documented in `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md` as DEC-001 through DEC-015. Each decision has id, question, options_considered, choice, consequences, and projects_to populated, e.g., DEC-011-privacy-surfaces and DEC-013-audit-response-truth show concrete projections to specific files.

DEC-012 references `decisions-pending.md` for target dates, and the projects_to lists for DEC-012/DEC-015 cite it, but the evidence bundle does not show the contents of `docs/decisions/decisions-pending.md`. Cannot verify each pending entry has owner, target_date, and a blocker description as the rubric requires.

Every decision's consequences section leads with explicit removal/elimination language. Examples: DEC-010 'Removes the need for application code to perform per-table tenant filtering; PostgreSQL RLS is the single runtime gate', DEC-011 'Eliminates per-file privacy guessing', DEC-013 'Eliminates hard-coded audit counts such as logged: 5 as acceptable evidence', DEC-015 'Removes the need to audit every Edge Function for hidden bypass behavior'. Each decision deletes work as required.

Dictionary fields all carry `decision_id` (verified by `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py` which fails if decision_ids don't match real decisions). However, RLS policies in `supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql` and Edge Functions in `supabase/functions/*/index.ts` show no inline comments naming the decision they implement (e.g., the policy `demo_read_organizations` does not cite DEC-010, and `audited-roster-read/index.ts` does not cite DEC-013). The pg_policies snapshot likewise lacks decision references. Only the dictionary half of the citation requirement is enforced.

DEC-013 (audit response truth) is now matched: `audited-roster-read/index.ts` reads back from `audit_log` after the RPC call and returns `logged: auditRows.length` plus `fields: auditRows.map(...)` — no hard-coded counts. DEC-010 (tenant_id JWT predicate, no using(true)) is matched in `pg_policies.snapshot.json` where every policy uses `tenant_id = COALESCE(...auth.jwt()...tenant_id...)`. DEC-011 (static mirrors may include synthetic directory data) is consistent with `site/api/people.json` carrying email from the synthetic North Valley tenant. DEC-014 (mirrors generated from same seed) is implemented by `scripts/build_static_api.py`.

`docs/dictionary-coverage-matrix.md` and DEC-012-runtime-coverage-per-spec agree: OneRoster core marked runnable (with hosted Supabase REST + RLS + tests), QTI and CASE marked 'No' for runnable slice (doc-only), Caliper/LTI/Security marked 'Partial: authenticated Edge Function receipt path'. The matrix correctly distinguishes generated dictionaries from runtime backing, matching the decision's claim about each spec's posture.

`tests/supabase_tenant_rls_test.py` creates two tenant-scoped Auth users with distinct `app_metadata.tenant_id` claims and verifies cross-tenant reads are blocked through the live REST URL. `tests/supabase_audit_log_test.py` calls the live `audited-roster-read` function and reads back `audit_log` rows verifying `client_id`, `scope`, `purpose`, `field_accessed`, `tenant_id`, and `timestamp`. Portal `site/index.html` shows copy-pasteable curl against `qzxlgrerjoiamxvnkklq.supabase.co`, and `cd demo && npm run reset-db && npm test` is in VERIFY.md. Runtime prereqs report all credentials present.

The portal's #decisions section in `site/index.html` is rendered by `site/app.js` from a hardcoded array (`const decisions = [...]` with titles like 'Canonical Data Model', 'SQL Contract', 'Identifiers') that bears no relationship to the actual DEC-001 through DEC-015 register in `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md`. There are no `#dec-001-person-agent-subject` anchors, no rendered `consequences` or `projects_to` sections per decision, and the real decisions are only reachable via a footer link to a single rendered Markdown page (`docs/decisions-standards-overlap-decisions.html`) — exactly the 'one big Markdown dump' the substance check forbids.

`scripts/evaluate_platform.py` is invoked at the end of VERIFY.md and writes `site/api/platform-evaluation.json` with rubricVersion, counts, done flag, and per-item items. However, the report schema in `evaluate_platform.py` (lines defining `report = {...}`) contains no `projections` section summarizing decision-to-reality matches; it only emits items, counts, and `runtimePrereqStatus`. The substance_check explicitly says 'Report missing the projections summary fails'.

`scripts/codex_loop.py` defines `read_overrides()` which loads `docs/loop-overrides.md`, `overrides_paused()` which detects a bare `PAUSE` token outside HTML comments, and `format_overrides_for_prompt()` which injects content under a 'Human overrides' section in the iteration prompt. The PAUSE check runs at the top of each iteration before any Codex call, halting the loop before the next iteration runs as required.

Termination logic in `scripts/codex_loop.py` references the LLM evaluator's done flag: `if after_eval and after_eval.get('done'): print('Evaluator reports done...'); return 0`. There is also a stall-streak safety rail and max-iterations cap, but no numeric-threshold gate on the advisory spec score. The early-exit before iteration 1 also checks `before_eval.get('done')`.

advisoryScoreGate.note: Advisory only. The deterministic score gate no longer gates publish; this evaluator does.
